sections.paragraph:
προφυλάσσω fut. mid. προφυλάξομαι 2 Km 22:24 (φύλαξ; Hom. Hymns, Hdt. et al.; SIG 730, 15; PWürzburg 9, 8 [II a.d.])

① to take security measures beforehand, guard, protect τινά someone, act. ITr 8:1. πρ. τινὰ ἀπό τινος someone against someone ISm 4:1 (on ἀπό τινος cp. 2 Km 22:24). S. also 2.

② to be vigilant in the face of danger, be on one’s guard, mid. (Hdt., Thu. et al.; 2 Km 22:24; SibOr 3, 733) w. μή and inf. IMg 11 (though because of the analogy of the two passages mentioned above the mid. here could have act. mng. [Diogenian. the Epicurean, of II a.d., Fgm. 4 Gercke: in Eus., PE 4, 3, 7 end, twice; and Philo, Cher. 34 the mid. means ‘ward someth. off’]; Rdm.2 79f).—DELG s.v. φύλαξ.

fut. fut. = fut.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

act. act. = active

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

inf. inf. = infinitive

IMg IMg = Ignatius to the Magnesians—List 1

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

Rdm. Rdm. = LRadermacher, Neutestamentliche Grammatik—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 891
